papers.title: High-strength Fe–20Mn–Al–C-based Alloys with Low Density
papers.paper_scope: Investigation of mechanical properties, density and microstructure of Fe-20Mn-Al-C and Fe-20Mn-Al-C-5Cr low-density alloys, focusing on specific strength and κ-carbide precipitation hardening through control of composition and cooling rate.

processes.description: Annealed at 1100°C for 15 min followed by water quenching (cooling rate ~1100°C/s)
Annealed at 1100°C for 15 min followed by air cooling (cooling rate ~15°C/s)
Annealed at 1000°C for 15 min followed by water quenching
Annealed at 1000°C for 15 min followed by air cooling
Annealed at 900°C for 15 min followed by water quenching
Annealed at 900°C for 15 min followed by air cooling
Cold rolled with 30% reduction after annealing at 1100°C for 15 min and water quenching
Cold rolled with 92% reduction after annealing at 1100°C for 15 min and water quenching
Cold rolled with 93% reduction after annealing at 1100°C for 15 min and water quenching
Cold rolled with 94% reduction after annealing at 1100°C for 15 min and water quenching

structures.overall_structure: γ matrix with very fine nano-size κ precipitates

precipitates.distribution: very fine precipitates slightly confirmed by dark field imaging of (100)κ superlattice reflection

structures.overall_structure: γ matrix with cuboidal κ precipitates aligned along <001>

precipitates.distribution: cuboidal precipitates aligned along <001> directions; superlattice reflections much stronger than WQ

interfaces.phase_evolution: κ-carbide precipitates during cooling from annealing temperature; cuboidal morphology aligned along <001> in air-cooled specimen suggests crystallographic alignment with γ matrix analogous to Ni-based superalloys γ/γ'

comparative_performance.benchmark_alloy: conventional austenitic steels and Fe-Mn-Si-Al TWIP steels

characterization_methods.purpose: Phase identification and microstructure observation at room temperature
characterization_methods.key_findings: Identified γ, γ+α, γ+κ and α phases in Fe-Mn-Al-C(-Cr) alloys annealed at 1100°C; confirmed γ single phase and γ+κ/γ+α two-phase regions

characterization_methods.technique: Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) and selected area diffraction
characterization_methods.purpose: Observation of κ-carbide precipitation in water-quenched specimen
characterization_methods.key_findings: Very fine κ precipitates slightly confirmed; extra spots between fundamental reflections indicating perovskite structure; intensity of superlattice reflections lower than air-cooled

characterization_methods.technique: Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) and dark field imaging
characterization_methods.purpose: Observation of κ-carbide precipitation in air-cooled specimen
characterization_methods.key_findings: Cuboidal κ phase precipitates aligned along <001> directions clearly observed in dark field of (100)κ superlattice reflection; superlattice reflections intensity much higher than water-quenched
Hardness increased with increasing Al and C contents; air-cooled specimens exhibited higher hardness than water-quenched due to κ precipitation
Yield strength, tensile strength and elongation measured at strain rate 0.5 mm/min using dumbbell-shaped specimens; specific strength calculated from tensile strength and density

characterization_methods.purpose: Check for stress-induced martensite after tensile deformation
characterization_methods.key_findings: No stress-induced martensite phase detected in tensile deformed specimen

computational_details.validation_against_experiment: Calculated density agreed with Archimedes measurement, e.g., calculated 6.64 vs observed 6.67 g/cm3 for Fe-20Mn-10Al-5Cr-1.5C alloy

analytical_model.equation_role: Estimation of alloy density from pure element fcc density parameters and atomic fractions
analytical_model.model_note: Linear mixture rule using atomic fractions; density parameters evaluated by extrapolation from binary fcc alloys; applicable to γ single phase and extended to other phases

computational_details.validation_against_experiment: Calculated equilibrium phase diagram of Fe-20Mn-Al-C-5Cr at 1100°C agreed with OM observation results

unmapped_findings: Fig. 4 provides typical stress–strain curves for quaternary and quinary alloys; exact numerical stress–strain pairs beyond approximate figure annotations are not provided in the text and require image pixel access.
Cold-workability parameter W was defined as W = (t0 - tmin)/t0 × 100%, where t0 is initial thickness and tmin is minimum thickness before crack appearance during room-temperature cold-rolling.

Fig. 8 presents a comparative plot of specific strength versus tensile elongation for the present alloys against conventional austenitic, ferritic, martensitic, precipitation hardened, dual-phase, super toughness and Fe-Mn-Si-Al TWIP steels.

The present Fe-Mn-Al-C(-Cr) alloys exhibit higher specific strength than conventional steels and TWIP steels, though TRIP steels reported by Zakay et al. possess higher specific strength via complicated thermomechanical processing.
Density parameters used in estimation: D_Fe^fcc = 8.19, D_Mn^fcc = 7.19, D_Al^fcc = 2.70, D_Cr^fcc = 6.36, D_C^fcc = 2.85 (g/cm3).
Hot-rolling was performed at 1200°C to sheets of about 1.5 mm thickness prior to heat treatment and mechanical testing.
